metadata.title: SpyCloud Sentinel — Unified Deployment
metadata.description: Single-template deployment for the complete SpyCloud Threat Intelligence integration with Microsoft Sentinel. Includes: CCF data connector, custom tables, Key Vault for secrets, MDE/CA remediation playbooks, analytics rules, automation rules, action groups for notifications, and optional Entra ID log connectors.
metadata.author: SpyCloud

metadata.description: PRIMARY AZURE REGION — This single selection determines the region for ALL deployed resources: the Log Analytics workspace, Data Collection Endpoint (DCE), Data Collection Rule (DCR), custom tables, and all Sentinel solution artifacts. Defaults to your resource group's region. Only change this if you need resources in a different region than your resource group. IMPORTANT: If you have an existing Log Analytics workspace, select the region where it is already deployed. The Azure Portal will dynamically show all available regions for your subscription — no hardcoded list needed.

OPTIONAL — Override the workspace region if it differs from the primary deployment region above. Leave BLANK to use the primary deployment region for everything (recommended for most deployments). Only set this if your Log Analytics workspace is in a different region than your resource group. When set, all workspace-scoped resources (tables, connectors, DCR, DCE) will deploy to this region instead.

Azure Subscription ID where Microsoft Sentinel is deployed. Auto-detected from your current context. Only override this for cross-subscription deployments where Sentinel is in a different subscription than where you are deploying from.

Resource group containing the Microsoft Sentinel workspace. Auto-detected from your current context. All SpyCloud resources (DCE, DCR, custom tables, connectors) will be deployed into this resource group. Only override if your Sentinel workspace is in a different resource group.

REQUIRED — Name of the Log Analytics workspace for Microsoft Sentinel. If createNewWorkspace is TRUE: enter your desired name (e.g., 'spycloud-supreme-sentinel'). If createNewWorkspace is FALSE: enter the exact name of your existing workspace. To find existing workspaces: Azure Portal → Log Analytics workspaces, or CLI: az monitor log-analytics workspace list --query '[].name' -o tsv

Name for the Data Collection Endpoint (DCE) that receives SpyCloud API data. Default: 'dce-spycloud-{workspaceName}' — follows Azure naming best practices. The DCE is the HTTPS ingestion endpoint that the SpyCloud connector sends data to before it reaches your workspace. If a DCE with this name already exists in your resource group, it will be reused. Example: If workspace is 'law-sentinel-prod', DCE will be 'dce-spycloud-law-sentinel-prod'.

Name for the Data Collection Rule (DCR) that transforms and routes SpyCloud data to the correct tables. Default: 'dcr-spycloud-{workspaceName}' — follows Azure naming best practices. The DCR contains KQL transforms that normalize incoming SpyCloud API data and route it to the appropriate custom table (Watchlist, Breach Catalog, MDE Logs, or Conditional Access Logs). Example: If workspace is 'law-sentinel-prod', DCR will be 'dcr-spycloud-law-sentinel-prod'.

Number of days to retain SpyCloud data in Log Analytics custom tables. Applies to ALL four SpyCloud tables. • 30 days = Minimum retention (GDPR-friendly, lowest storage cost) • 60 days = Short-term investigation window • 90 days = RECOMMENDED — supports most SOC investigation workflows and SOC2/NIST baselines • 180 days = Extended — trend analysis, recurring breach pattern detection • 365 days = Full year — required for PCI DSS, annual SOC2 audits, some cyber insurance policies • 730 days = Maximum (2 years) — legal hold, regulatory investigations, litigation support COST IMPACT: Each additional 30 days adds ~1x your monthly ingestion volume to storage costs. For HIPAA (6-year requirement), use 730 days here plus Azure Log Analytics archive/export for long-term.

Azure resource tags applied to ALL deployed resources (DCE, DCR, tables, connectors). Use tags for cost tracking, environment labeling, compliance labeling, and resource governance. Default tags include solution name, vendor, data classification, and management method. Add your organization's required tags (costCenter, owner, project, department) as key-value pairs. Example: Add 'costCenter': 'SEC-OPS-001' and 'owner': 'soc-team@company.com' for chargebacks.

Pricing tier of your Log Analytics workspace (informational — does not change your workspace tier). • PerGB2018 = Pay-As-You-Go (most common, ~$2.76/GB ingested) • CapacityReservation = Committed tier with discounts (100GB/day+, saves 10-25%) • Free = Limited to 500MB/day ingestion (not recommended for SpyCloud) Typical SpyCloud ingestion: 1-50 MB/day (small), 50-500 MB/day (large enterprise). Check Azure Portal → Log Analytics → Usage and estimated costs for your current tier.

Public network access setting for the Data Collection Endpoint (DCE). • 'Enabled' (DEFAULT) = DCE accepts data from the public internet (standard for cloud-to-cloud connectors) • 'Disabled' = DCE only accepts data via Private Link (requires Azure Private Link configuration) For most SpyCloud deployments, 'Enabled' is correct because the Sentinel CCF connector sends data from Azure's internal infrastructure to the DCE. Only set to 'Disabled' if your security policy mandates Private Link for all Monitor ingestion.
Auto-generated deployment timestamp in UTC. Do not modify — ARM populates this automatically at deployment time.
Deploy the MDE Remediation Playbook (device isolation, tagging, IOC submission). Requires Microsoft Defender for Endpoint P2 license.
Deploy the Conditional Access Remediation Playbook (password reset, session revocation, CA group assignment). Requires Entra ID P1+ license.
Deploy the SpyCloud Infostealer Detection analytics rule. Creates Sentinel incidents when severity >= 20 exposures are detected.
Deploy the automation rule that connects the analytics rule to the playbooks for automatic remediation on incident creation.

OPTIONAL — Object ID of the Entra ID security group to add compromised users to. This group should be targeted by a Conditional Access policy that restricts access. Create the group first: Entra ID → Groups → New group → Security. Leave blank to skip CA group assignment.
Device isolation type for MDE remediation. 'Full' = complete network isolation (recommended for infostealer response). 'Selective' = allow Outlook, Teams, and Defender communication.
Tag name applied to compromised devices in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint. Used for tracking and filtering in the MDE portal.
Severity level assigned to Sentinel incidents created by the SpyCloud infostealer analytics rule.
How often the analytics rule runs. PT1H = every hour (recommended). PT5M = every 5 minutes (near real-time, higher cost).
Minimum SpyCloud severity to trigger remediation. 20 = infostealer credentials (recommended). 2 = all breaches (high volume). 25 = infostealer + stolen sessions only.
Create an Azure Monitor Action Group for email/webhook notifications when SpyCloud incidents are created or data ingestion stops.
OPTIONAL — Email address to receive alert notifications for SpyCloud incidents and data health issues. Required if enableNotifications is TRUE.
OPTIONAL — Microsoft Teams incoming webhook URL for alert notifications. Create one in Teams: Channel → Connectors → Incoming Webhook.

Deploy the FULL library of SpyCloud analytics rules (all created DISABLED by default for review). Includes: infostealer detection, plaintext password alerts, PII/SSN exposure alerts, session cookie theft, device reinfection, multi-domain credential exposure, geographic anomaly, executive/VIP exposure, remediation gap detection, and high-sighting credential alerts. You must manually enable each rule after reviewing its logic.
Deploy analytics rules specifically for detecting stolen session cookies and tokens (severity 25). These exposures enable MFA bypass via session hijacking and require immediate session revocation.

Deploy analytics rules that correlate SpyCloud exposure data with third-party identity provider logs (Okta, Duo, Ping Identity, CyberArk) if those logs are already ingested into Sentinel via their respective connectors. These rules detect compromised credentials being used across IdP sign-in events.

Set to TRUE to create a new Log Analytics workspace AND enable Microsoft Sentinel on it. Set to FALSE if you already have a workspace with Sentinel enabled. When TRUE, a new workspace is created using the name in the 'workspace' parameter.
LEAVE BLANK on first deploy. Get after deployment via post-deploy.sh or Azure Portal.
LEAVE BLANK on first deploy. Get after deployment via post-deploy.sh or Azure Portal.

This parameter is reserved. Post-deployment configuration (DCE/DCR resolution, RBAC, API permissions) is handled by scripts/post-deploy.sh or scripts/deploy-all.sh. Run one of these scripts after deployment completes.

Deploy the Credential Exposure → Automated Identity Response playbook. Responds to exposed credentials by enforcing MFA via Conditional Access, checking sign-in activity, alerting SOC via Teams, and logging state changes. Requires: Security Administrator role, Graph IdentityRisk.ReadWrite.All, Conditional Access Admin.

Deploy the SpyCloud Threat Feed → MDE Blocklist playbook. Runs on a schedule (every 2-4 hours) to match severity 25 infostealer records against Microsoft Defender for Endpoint devices, creates enriched tables with both SpyCloud and MDE data, and offers automated isolation or alerting. Requires: MDE API enabled, Defender for Endpoint license.

Microsoft Teams incoming webhook URL for SOC alert notifications. Used by the Credential Response playbook to send real-time alerts to your security operations channel. Leave blank to skip Teams integration.
ServiceNow instance URL (e.g., https://your-instance.service-now.com) for automatic ticket creation. Leave blank to skip ServiceNow integration.
How often (in hours) the MDE Blocklist playbook runs to scan for new severity 25 infostealer matches against Defender for Endpoint. Default: every 4 hours.

Deploy the SpyCloud Threat Intelligence Dashboard workbook in Sentinel. Provides real-time charts for exposure overview, severity trends, top users, infected devices, password analysis, remediation status, and breach catalog.
Environment tag applied to all resources.
Owner tag — team or individual responsible.
Cost center tag for billing.
Pricing tier for new Log Analytics workspace (only when createNewWorkspace=TRUE). PerGB2018=Pay-As-You-Go, CapacityReservation=committed discounts.

Slack Incoming Webhook URL for SOC notifications. Logic Apps will send alerts here when incidents are created. Leave blank to skip Slack integration. Create at: api.slack.com/apps → Incoming Webhooks.
Jira Automation webhook URL for auto-creating issues on credential exposure. Leave blank to skip.
Azure DevOps Service Hook URL for auto-creating work items. Leave blank to skip.

columns.name: infected_path
columns.description: File system path on the infected device where the malware or infostealer was found (e.g., C:\Users\victim\AppData\Roaming\malware.exe).

columns.name: user_hostname
columns.description: Hostname of the compromised machine as reported by the operating system at the time of infection.

columns.name: ip_addresses
columns.description: Array of IP addresses associated with the infected machine at the time credentials were stolen.

columns.name: timezone
columns.description: System timezone of the infected machine (e.g., UTC-5, America/New_York).

columns.name: target_url
columns.description: Full URL of the website or application where stolen credentials were captured (login page, form submission endpoint, etc.).

columns.name: document_id
columns.description: SpyCloud unique identifier for this individual exposure record. Use for deduplication and record-level lookups.

columns.name: target_domain
columns.description: Top-level domain of the target site where credentials were compromised (e.g., example.com).

columns.name: user_sys_registered_owner
columns.description: Registered owner name from the infected machine's operating system registration.

columns.name: display_resolution
columns.description: Screen display resolution of the infected device (e.g., 1920x1080), useful for device fingerprinting.

columns.name: password
columns.description: Hashed or redacted password associated with the compromised credential. See password_plaintext for cleartext when enabled.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Email address of the compromised identity. Primary key for correlating exposures to corporate users.

columns.name: severity
columns.description: SpyCloud severity score: 2=Credential Breach, 5=Breach+PII, 20=Infostealer, 25=Infostealer+Application. Higher values indicate more actionable and dangerous exposures.

columns.name: av_softwares
columns.description: Array of antivirus/security software installed on the infected machine at time of compromise. Useful for evaluating endpoint protection gaps.

columns.name: log_id
columns.description: Identifier for the infostealer malware log that contained this credential record.

columns.name: user_os
columns.description: Operating system and version of the infected machine (e.g., Windows 10 Pro 21H2).

columns.name: email_username
columns.description: Local part of the email address (portion before the @ symbol).

columns.name: spycloud_publish_date
columns.description: Date when SpyCloud published this exposure record to customers. Use for tracking recency of threat intelligence.

columns.name: infected_machine_id
columns.description: SpyCloud-generated unique identifier for the infected device. Use to group all credentials stolen from the same machine.

columns.name: password_plaintext
columns.description: Cleartext password when available and when show_plain_password is enabled. Handle with extreme care per your organization's data handling policy.

columns.name: infected_time
columns.description: Estimated date/time when the device was infected by the infostealer malware.

columns.name: country_code
columns.description: Two-letter ISO country code of the infected machine's geographic location.

columns.name: keyboard_languages
columns.description: Keyboard language layouts configured on the infected device (e.g., en-US, ru-RU).

columns.name: password_type
columns.description: Type of password stored: plaintext, hashed (MD5, SHA1, SHA256, bcrypt, etc.), or unknown.

columns.name: domain
columns.description: Corporate domain associated with the compromised email address (e.g., spycloud.com).

columns.name: email_domain
columns.description: Domain portion of the compromised email address (after the @ symbol).

columns.name: source_id
columns.description: Numeric identifier linking this exposure to a specific breach in the SpyCloud Breach Catalog. Join with SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL.source_id.

columns.name: target_subdomain
columns.description: Subdomain of the target site where credentials were compromised (e.g., login.example.com → login).

columns.name: sighting
columns.description: Number of times SpyCloud has observed this credential pair across different breach sources.

columns.name: account_last_activity_time
columns.description: Last activity timestamp on the compromised account as captured in the breach data.

columns.name: account_notes
columns.description: Additional notes or metadata associated with the compromised account from the breach source.

columns.name: account_signup_time
columns.description: Signup or registration timestamp of the compromised account.

columns.name: username
columns.description: Username (non-email) used for the compromised account.

columns.name: homepage
columns.description: Homepage or profile URL associated with the compromised account.

columns.name: salt
columns.description: Cryptographic salt used in password hashing, if available from the breach data.

columns.name: account_login_time
columns.description: Last login timestamp of the compromised account as captured in the breach data.

columns.name: social_other
columns.description: Array of additional social media profile links or identifiers associated with the compromised identity.

columns.name: last_name
columns.description: Last name of the compromised individual as found in the breach data.

columns.name: full_name
columns.description: Full name of the compromised individual.

columns.name: phone
columns.description: Phone number associated with the compromised identity.

columns.name: first_name
columns.description: First name of the compromised individual.

columns.name: address_1
columns.description: Primary street address of the compromised individual.

columns.name: account_modification_time
columns.description: Timestamp when the compromised account was last modified.

columns.name: gender
columns.description: Gender of the compromised individual as found in the breach data.

columns.name: city
columns.description: City of the compromised individual's address.

columns.name: birth_year
columns.description: Birth year of the compromised individual.

columns.name: dob
columns.description: Full date of birth of the compromised individual.

columns.name: company_website
columns.description: Company website URL associated with the compromised individual.

columns.name: industry
columns.description: Industry sector of the compromised individual's employer or organization.

columns.name: account_status
columns.description: Status of the compromised account (e.g., active, suspended, deactivated) as captured in the breach.

columns.name: social_crunchbase
columns.description: Crunchbase profile URL or identifier associated with the compromised individual.

columns.name: company_name
columns.description: Employer or company name of the compromised individual.

columns.name: social_linkedin
columns.description: LinkedIn profile URL or identifier associated with the compromised identity.

columns.name: social_twitter
columns.description: Twitter/X profile URL or identifier associated with the compromised identity.

columns.name: account_caption
columns.description: Account bio, tagline, or caption from the compromised profile.

columns.name: address_2
columns.description: Secondary address line (suite, apartment, building) of the compromised individual.

columns.name: ssn_last_four
columns.description: Last four digits of the Social Security Number found in the breach data. Indicates severe PII exposure.

columns.name: social_security_number
columns.description: Full Social Security Number found in the breach data. Indicates critical PII exposure requiring immediate notification.

columns.name: health_insurance_id
columns.description: Health insurance identifier found in the breach data. May trigger HIPAA notification requirements.

columns.name: postal_code
columns.description: Postal/ZIP code of the compromised individual.

columns.name: age
columns.description: Age of the compromised individual at the time of the breach.

columns.name: taxid
columns.description: Tax identification number found in the breach data.

columns.name: state
columns.description: State or province of the compromised individual.

columns.name: account_type
columns.description: Type or tier of the compromised account (e.g., admin, user, premium).

columns.name: device_name
columns.description: Name(s) of device(s) associated with the compromised identity.

columns.name: bank_number
columns.description: Bank account number found in the breach data. Indicates financial data exposure.

columns.name: mobile_equipment_id
columns.description: IMEI or mobile equipment identifier of the device associated with the exposure.

columns.name: country
columns.description: Country of the compromised individual.

columns.name: educational_institution
columns.description: Educational institution associated with the compromised individual.

columns.name: job_title
columns.description: Job title of the compromised individual.

columns.name: account_image_url
columns.description: URL of the account profile image from the breached service.

columns.name: student_id
columns.description: Student ID number found in the breach data.

columns.name: account_id
columns.description: Account identifier from the breached service.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp when the MDE remediation action was executed (UTC).

columns.name: IncidentId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the SpyCloud-triggered remediation incident.

columns.name: DocumentId
columns.description: SpyCloud document ID of the exposure record that triggered this remediation action.

columns.name: SpyCloudPublishDate
columns.description: Date when SpyCloud published the exposure record that initiated this remediation.

columns.name: InfectedTime
columns.description: Estimated time of the original infostealer infection on the compromised device.

columns.name: BreachTitle
columns.description: Name of the SpyCloud breach source associated with this remediation action.

columns.name: InfectedMachineId
columns.description: SpyCloud unique identifier for the compromised device being remediated.

columns.name: HostName
columns.description: Hostname of the device in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint.

columns.name: NormalizedHostName
columns.description: Normalized (lowercase, trimmed) hostname for consistent matching across systems.

columns.name: DeviceId
columns.description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint device identifier.

columns.name: OSPlatform
columns.description: Operating system platform of the device (e.g., Windows10, WindowsServer2019).

columns.name: RiskScore
columns.description: Microsoft Defender risk score for the device (Low, Medium, High, Critical).

columns.name: ExposureLevel
columns.description: Microsoft Defender exposure level indicating the device's vulnerability posture.

columns.name: IsolationRequested
columns.description: Whether network isolation was requested for the compromised device.

columns.name: IsolationStatus
columns.description: Whether network isolation was successfully applied to the device.

columns.name: MachineTagAdded
columns.description: Whether a SpyCloud tag was added to the device in Microsoft Defender.

columns.name: MachineTagName
columns.description: Name of the tag applied to the device (e.g., SpyCloud-Compromised, SpyCloud-Infostealer).

columns.name: AddedIOCsToDefender
columns.description: Whether indicators of compromise (IOCs) from the exposure were submitted to Microsoft Defender.

columns.name: PlaybookName
columns.description: Name of the automated remediation playbook that executed this action.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp when the Conditional Access remediation action was executed (UTC).

columns.name: IncidentId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the SpyCloud-triggered identity remediation incident.

columns.name: DocumentId
columns.description: SpyCloud document ID of the exposure record that triggered this identity remediation.

columns.name: SpyCloudPublishDate
columns.description: Date when SpyCloud published the exposure that initiated this identity protection action.

columns.name: InfectedTime
columns.description: Estimated time of the original credential compromise or infostealer infection.

columns.name: BreachTitle
columns.description: Name of the SpyCloud breach source associated with this remediation.

columns.name: InfectedMachineId
columns.description: SpyCloud unique identifier for the infected device linked to this compromised identity.

columns.name: Username
columns.description: Azure AD / Entra ID username of the compromised user account.

columns.name: UserEmail
columns.description: Email address of the compromised user whose identity remediation was triggered.

columns.name: ForcedPasswordResetOnNextSignIn
columns.description: Whether the user was forced to reset their password at next sign-in.

columns.name: UserSessionsRevoked
columns.description: Whether all active user sessions and refresh tokens were revoked.

columns.name: CAgroup
columns.description: Whether the user was added to a Conditional Access exclusion/restriction security group.

columns.name: UserDisabled
columns.description: Whether the user account was disabled in Azure AD / Entra ID.

columns.name: PlaybookName
columns.description: Name of the automated identity remediation playbook that executed this action.

dataFlows.transformKql:
source
| extend TimeGenerated = now(),
    breach_title = tostring(['title']),
    source_id = toint(id),
    breach_type = tostring(['type']),
    num_records = toint(num_records),
    acquisition_date = tostring(acquisition_date),
    spycloud_publish_date = tostring(spycloud_publish_date),
    site = tostring(site),
    site_description = tostring(site_description),
    confidence = toint(confidence),
    uuid = tostring(uuid)
| project TimeGenerated, breach_title, description, status, source_id,
    breach_type, num_records, acquisition_date, spycloud_publish_date,
    site, site_description, confidence, uuid

source
| extend
    TimeGenerated = todatetime(TimeGenerated),
    IncidentId = tostring(IncidentId),
    DocumentId = tostring(DocumentId),
    SpyCloudPublishDate = todatetime(SpyCloudPublishDate),
    InfectedTime = tostring(InfectedTime),
    BreachTitle = tostring(BreachTitle),
    InfectedMachineId = tostring(InfectedMachineId),
    HostName = tostring(HostName),
    NormalizedHostName = tostring(NormalizedHostName),
    DeviceId = tostring(DeviceId),
    OSPlatform = tostring(OSPlatform),
    RiskScore = tostring(RiskScore),
    ExposureLevel = tostring(ExposureLevel),
    IsolationRequested = tobool(IsolationRequested),
    IsolationStatus = tobool(IsolationStatus),
    MachineTagAdded = tobool(MachineTagAdded),
    MachineTagName = tostring(MachineTagName),
    AddedIOCsToDefender = tobool(AddedIOCsToDefender),
    PlaybookName = tostring(PlaybookName)
| project
    TimeGenerated, IncidentId, DocumentId, SpyCloudPublishDate,
    InfectedTime, BreachTitle, InfectedMachineId, HostName,
    NormalizedHostName, DeviceId, OSPlatform, RiskScore,
    ExposureLevel, IsolationRequested, IsolationStatus,
    MachineTagAdded, MachineTagName, AddedIOCsToDefender,
    PlaybookName

source
| extend
    TimeGenerated = todatetime(TimeGenerated),
    IncidentId = tostring(IncidentId),
    DocumentId = tostring(DocumentId),
    SpyCloudPublishDate = todatetime(SpyCloudPublishDate),
    InfectedTime = tostring(InfectedTime),
    BreachTitle = tostring(BreachTitle),
    InfectedMachineId = tostring(InfectedMachineId),
    Username = tostring(Username),
    UserEmail = tostring(UserEmail),
    ForcedPasswordResetOnNextSignIn = tobool(ForcedPasswordResetOnNextSignIn),
    UserSessionsRevoked = tobool(UserSessionsRevoked),
    CAgroup = tobool(CAgroup),
    UserDisabled = tobool(UserDisabled),
    PlaybookName = tostring(PlaybookName)
| project
    TimeGenerated, IncidentId, DocumentId, SpyCloudPublishDate,
    InfectedTime, BreachTitle, InfectedMachineId, Username,
    UserEmail, ForcedPasswordResetOnNextSignIn, UserSessionsRevoked,
    CAgroup, UserDisabled, PlaybookName

connectorUiConfig.title: SpyCloud Breach Watchlist/Catalog
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown:
![SpyCloud](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/Azure-Sentinel/master/Logos/SpyCloud_Enterprise_Protection.svg)

The **SpyCloud Threat Intelligence Connector** ingests breach and infostealer exposure data from SpyCloud's Enterprise API into Microsoft Sentinel, enabling automated identity protection and endpoint remediation workflows.

**Data streams include:**
- **Watchlist Exposures** — Compromised credentials, PII, and infected device records associated with your organization's monitored email domains, IP ranges, and assets
- **Breach Catalog** — Metadata for all breach sources in SpyCloud's database, enabling contextual enrichment and correlation
- **MDE Remediation Logs** — Audit trail of automated Microsoft Defender for Endpoint actions (device isolation, tagging, IOC submission)
- **Conditional Access Logs** — Audit trail of automated Azure AD / Entra ID identity protection actions (password resets, session revocations, account disablement)

**Use cases:** Compromised credential detection, infostealer infection response, automated password reset enforcement, post-infection device isolation, breach impact assessment, darknet exposure monitoring, compliance evidence collection, third-party/supply chain risk assessment, VIP/executive exposure monitoring.

sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Exposure Count by Corporate Email Domain
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(email_domain)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), UniqueEmails = dcount(email) by email_domain
| sort by ExposureCount desc

sampleQueries.description: Compromised Credentials with Plaintext Passwords (Requires show_plain_password=True)
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(password_plaintext)
| project TimeGenerated, email, target_domain, password_type, severity, source_id
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Infected Devices Grouped by Machine ID and Hostname
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(infected_machine_id)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(target_domain), LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated) by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, user_os
| sort by ExposureCount desc

sampleQueries.description: Exposures Enriched with Breach Catalog Metadata
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| join kind=inner (
    SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL
    | project source_id, breach_title, breach_type, confidence, status
) on source_id
| project TimeGenerated, email, target_domain, severity, breach_title, breach_type, confidence
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Recent Breaches from Catalog with Record Counts
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| project TimeGenerated, source_id, breach_title, breach_type, num_records, confidence, status
| take 50

sampleQueries.description: MDE Remediation Actions — Device Isolation & Tagging Audit
sampleQueries.query:
Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL
| project TimeGenerated, IncidentId, HostName, DeviceId, OSPlatform,
    IsolationRequested, IsolationStatus, MachineTagAdded, MachineTagName,
    AddedIOCsToDefender, PlaybookName, BreachTitle
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Conditional Access Remediation — Password Resets & Session Revocations
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL
| project TimeGenerated, IncidentId, Username, UserEmail,
    ForcedPasswordResetOnNextSignIn, UserSessionsRevoked,
    CAgroup, UserDisabled, PlaybookName, BreachTitle
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Top 10 Most-Targeted Domains by Exposure Volume
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(target_domain)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), UniqueEmails = dcount(email), AvgSeverity = avg(severity) by target_domain
| top 10 by ExposureCount desc

sampleQueries.description: Exposure Timeline — Daily Ingestion Volume by Severity
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| summarize Count = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d), severity
| render timechart

sampleQueries.description: Full Health Dashboard — All 4 SpyCloud Tables
sampleQueries.query:
union
  (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Watchlist'),
  (SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Breach Catalog'),
  (Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='MDE Remediation'),
  (SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Conditional Access')
| project Table, Records, Latest,
    HoursSinceUpdate = datetime_diff('hour', now(), Latest),
    Status = case(Records == 0, 'No Data', datetime_diff('hour', now(), Latest) > 24, 'Stale', 'Active')

instructionSteps.title: Prerequisites — Read Before Connecting
instructionSteps.description:
**Complete ALL prerequisites before clicking Connect.**

### A. SpyCloud Account & API Key

| Requirement | Details |
|------------|----------|
| Subscription | Active SpyCloud Enterprise or Enterprise+ API plan |
| API Key | Generate from [portal.spycloud.com](https://portal.spycloud.com) → **Settings** → **API Keys** |
| API Region | US (`api.spycloud.io`) — configured automatically |
| Products | Employee ATO, Consumer ATO, Compass, Third-Party Insight, Ransomware Prevention |

### B. Azure Permissions Required

| Role | Scope | Purpose |
|------|-------|----------|
| Microsoft Sentinel Contributor | Resource Group | Create/manage connectors, rules, workbooks |
| Log Analytics Contributor | Workspace | Create custom tables, manage retention |
| Monitoring Metrics Publisher | Data Collection Rule | Authorize data ingestion via DCE |

### C. Network Requirements

| Direction | Protocol | Destination | Port |
|-----------|----------|-------------|------|
| Outbound | HTTPS | `api.spycloud.io` | 443 |
| Outbound | HTTPS | `*.ingest.monitor.azure.com` | 443 |

No inbound rules required — this is a pull-based connector.

### D. Capacity Planning

| Environment | Assets | Daily Ingestion | Notes |
|-------------|--------|-----------------|-------|
| Small/POC | <1,000 | 1-10 MB/day | Standard settings work |
| Medium | 1K-10K | 10-100 MB/day | Default 40-min polling |
| Large | 10K-100K | 100-500 MB/day | Consider Enterprise+ |
| Very Large | 100K+ | 500+ MB/day | Contact SpyCloud support |

parameters.text: Your SpyCloud API key is at portal.spycloud.com → Settings → API Keys. Need an account? Contact support@spycloud.com.

instructionSteps.title: Step 1 — SpyCloud API Authentication
instructionSteps.description:
Enter your SpyCloud Enterprise API key. The key is sent as `X-Api-Key: <key>` on every API request and stored securely — never exposed in deployment logs or outputs.

**Where to find your API key:**
1. Open [portal.spycloud.com](https://portal.spycloud.com)
2. Click **Settings** (gear icon) → **API Keys**
3. Copy the key labeled **Enterprise API**
4. Paste it below

The key is sent as the `X-Api-Key` header on every API request.

instructionSteps.title: Step 2 — Severity Levels & Exposure Types
instructionSteps.description:
Control **what data** flows into Sentinel.

### Severity Level Reference

| Severity | Category | What It Contains | Response |
|----------|----------|------------------|---------|
| **2** | Breach Credential | Email + password from third-party breach | Monitor, reset if reused |
| **5** | Breach + PII | Above + name, phone, DOB, address, SSN | Notify affected users |
| **20** | Infostealer | Credential stolen by malware from infected device | **URGENT**: Isolate device, force reset |
| **25** | Infostealer + App | Above + stolen cookies, sessions, autofill | **CRITICAL**: Full remediation |

### Exposure Type Reference

| Type | Description | SpyCloud Product |
|------|-------------|------------------|
| **Corporate** | Credentials matching your monitored corporate email domains | Employee ATO Prevention |
| **Infected** | Credentials stolen by infostealer malware from compromised devices | Session Identity Protection |
| **Compass** | Consumer identity exposures for monitored consumer accounts | Consumer ATO Prevention |

**Recommendations:**
- **Maximum protection:** All severity levels + all exposure types
- **Infostealer focus:** Severity 20+25 only
- **Corporate only:** All severity levels, Corporate type only

options.text: Corporate — Your monitored email domains & IP assets
No — Exclude cleartext passwords (RECOMMENDED — most secure)
Yes — Include cleartext passwords (requires data handling policy + table RBAC)

parameters.text:
**Polling & Lookback Configuration**

Polling interval: 30 minutes (default). Data lookback: 365 days on first pull. The connector uses cursor-based pagination to handle large datasets. Rate limit: 2 queries/second with 5 retries.

instructionSteps.title: Step 3 — Data Streams & Tables
instructionSteps.description:
This connector creates and populates the following tables in your workspace:

### Connector Modules (activated automatically)

| Module | API Endpoint | Table | Description |
|--------|-------------|-------|-----------|
| Watchlist (New) | `/breach/data/watchlist` | `SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL` | Primary credential/PII exposure feed |
| Watchlist (Modified) | `/breach/data/watchlist` + modification dates | `SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL` | Updated/re-sighted records |
| Breach Catalog | `/breach/catalog` | `SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL` | Breach source metadata for enrichment |

### Audit Tables (populated by playbooks)

| Table | Columns | Populated By |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL` | 19 | MDE Remediation playbook (device isolation, tagging) |
| `SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL` | 14 | CA Identity playbook (password reset, session revoke) |

### Default Connector Settings

| Setting | Value | Notes |
|---------|-------|-------|
| API Region | US (`api.spycloud.io`) | Hardcoded — contact support for EU |
| Polling Interval | 40 minutes | Balanced freshness vs API consumption |
| Modified Records | Daily (1440 min) | Catches re-sighted credentials |
| Rate Limit | 2 QPS | Standard Enterprise tier |
| Retries | 3 | Exponential backoff |
| Timeout | 60 seconds | Increase if you have >50K assets |

parameters.text: All three connector modules activate automatically when you click Connect. No additional configuration needed for data streams.

### Polling & Retrieval Configuration

| Setting | Default | Range | Description |
|---------|---------|-------|-------------|
| **Polling Interval** | 30 min | 5–1440 min | How often new records are fetched from SpyCloud API |
| **Retry Count** | 5 | 1–10 | Number of retries on API call failure |
| **Timeout** | 120 sec | 30–300 sec | Per-request timeout before retry |
| **Lookback** | 365 days | 1–365 days | Historical depth on first data pull |

The Modified Records poller always uses a 24-hour window. All settings are passed to the CCF poller configuration and take effect immediately on next polling cycle.

instructionSteps.title: Step 4 — Use Cases & Integration
instructionSteps.description:
### What You Can Do With SpyCloud Data in Sentinel

| Use Case | How | Resources |
|----------|-----|-----------|
| **Compromised Credential Detection** | 22 analytics rules detect exposures by severity, type, and context | Sentinel → Analytics |
| **Automated Device Isolation** | MDE playbook isolates infected devices in Defender | Logic App: SpyCloud-MDE-Remediation |
| **Automated Password Reset** | CA playbook forces reset + revokes sessions in Entra ID | Logic App: SpyCloud-CA-Remediation |
| **SOC Alerting** | Teams/Slack notifications on critical exposures | Logic App: SpyCloud-CredResponse |
| **Scheduled Threat Scanning** | MDE Blocklist scans severity 25 on schedule | Logic App: SpyCloud-MDE-Blocklist |
| **Executive/VIP Monitoring** | Dedicated rule for CEO/CFO/CISO accounts | Analytics Rule #19 |
| **Password Reuse Detection** | Same hash across 3+ target domains | Analytics Rule #20 |
| **IdP Cross-Correlation** | Match exposures against Okta/Duo/Ping/Entra sign-ins | Analytics Rules #13-16 |
| **Breach Impact Assessment** | Join exposures with catalog for breach context | Analytics Rule #18 |
| **Interactive Investigation** | Security Copilot plugin with 28 KQL skills + AI agent | Copilot → Sources → SpyCloud |

### Recommended Additional Data Connectors

Enable these in Sentinel Content Hub for maximum correlation:

| Connector | Why | Enables |
|-----------|-----|---------|
| **Entra ID Diagnostics** | Sign-in correlation with compromised credentials | Rule #16, #17 |
| **Microsoft Defender XDR** | Alert timeline correlation with infections | MDE playbook enrichment |
| **Okta SSO** | Catch compromised creds in Okta sign-ins | Rule #13 |
| **Cisco Duo** | Catch compromised creds in Duo MFA | Rule #14 |
| **Microsoft 365** | Email/file access after credential compromise | Incident investigation |

parameters.text: After connecting, enable analytics rules: Sentinel → Analytics → filter 'SpyCloud' → enable individually. Start with Infostealer Exposure, Plaintext Password, Session Cookies, and Data Health.

instructionSteps.title: Step 5 — Connect
instructionSteps.description:
**Review your settings above, then click Connect.**

### What Happens When You Click Connect

1. **Validation** — API key verified against SpyCloud endpoint
2. **Backfill** — Initial 30-day historical data pull begins (5-30 minutes)
3. **Polling** — Ongoing 40-minute incremental polling activates
4. **Transform** — Raw API data normalized via KQL transforms in the DCR
5. **Ingestion** — Data flows into your custom tables
6. **Status** — Connector tile shows 'Connected' within 5-10 minutes

### Checklist Before Connecting

- [ ] API key from portal.spycloud.com → Settings → API Keys
- [ ] Severity levels selected (recommend: all 4)
- [ ] Exposure types selected (recommend: all 3)
- [ ] Password handling preference set (recommend: No / exclude)

### Resources Created by This Connector

| Resource | Name | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| DCE | `dce-spycloud-{workspace}` | HTTPS ingestion endpoint |
| DCR | `dcr-spycloud-{workspace}` | KQL transforms + table routing |
| Table | `SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL` | 73 columns — credentials, PII, device forensics |
| Table | `SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL` | 5 columns — breach source metadata |
| Table | `Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL` | 19 columns — MDE remediation audit |
| Table | `SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL` | 14 columns — CA remediation audit |

instructionSteps.title: Step 6 — Verification & Troubleshooting
instructionSteps.description:
### Quick Health Check (run 5-10 minutes after connecting)

```kusto
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| summarize TotalRecords=count(), Newest=max(TimeGenerated), Oldest=min(TimeGenerated), Domains=dcount(email_domain), Breaches=dcount(source_id)
| extend Status = iff(TotalRecords > 0, 'RECEIVING DATA', 'WAITING')
```

### Full 4-Table Health Dashboard

```kusto
union
  (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Watchlist'),
  (SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Catalog'),
  (Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='MDE'),
  (SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='CA')
| project Table, Records, Latest, HoursSince=datetime_diff('hour', now(), Latest)
```

### Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Cause | Fix |
|---------|-------|-----|
| No data after 30 min | Invalid API key | Regenerate at portal.spycloud.com → Settings → API Keys |
| No data after 30 min | Wrong severity filter | Ensure at least one severity level selected |
| Partial data | Rate limited (HTTP 429) | Contact SpyCloud to verify your API tier |
| Timeouts | Large watchlist | Contact SpyCloud support — may need Enterprise+ tier |
| Watchlist but no Catalog | Both should activate together | Disconnect and reconnect |
| No MDE/CA logs | Normal — these are populated by playbooks | Enable playbooks + create test incident |

### Support

| Issue | Contact |
|-------|----------|
| SpyCloud API / Data | support@spycloud.com or portal.spycloud.com |
| Azure / Sentinel | Azure Portal → Help + Support |
| This Integration | github.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL/issues |

properties.displayName: [variables('_solutionName')]
properties.descriptionHtml:
The SpyCloud Threat Intelligence Connector integrates SpyCloud's comprehensive breach and infostealer exposure data with Microsoft Sentinel, enabling security teams to detect compromised credentials, investigate infected devices, and automate identity protection responses.

Data Tables

  • SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL — Compromised credentials, PII, and infostealer-stolen data from your organization's monitored assets (email domains, IP ranges, executive watchlists)
  • SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL — Breach source metadata for contextual enrichment, correlation, and impact assessment
  • Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL — Audit log of automated Microsoft Defender for Endpoint remediation actions (device isolation, tagging, IOC submission)
  • SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL — Audit log of automated Azure AD / Entra ID actions (forced password resets, session revocations, Conditional Access group assignments, account disablement)

Key Use Cases

  • Compromised Credential Detection — Identify employee and customer credentials exposed in breaches or stolen by infostealer malware
  • Post-Infection Remediation — Automatically isolate infected devices and force credential resets when infostealer activity is detected
  • Darknet Exposure Monitoring — Continuously monitor for new exposures of corporate assets across SpyCloud's 600B+ recaptured data records
  • Account Takeover Prevention — Detect and block credential stuffing attempts using real-time exposure intelligence
  • Third-Party Risk Assessment — Monitor vendor and supply chain credential exposures for risk scoring
  • Compliance & Audit — Maintain evidence of credential exposure detection and automated remediation for regulatory requirements
  • VIP/Executive Protection — Prioritized monitoring of high-value targets for both credential and PII exposure

Requirements: SpyCloud Enterprise or Enterprise+ API subscription. Contact support@spycloud.com or visit portal.spycloud.com.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Infostealer Exposure Detected
properties.description: [concat('Detects new infostealer-sourced credential exposures from SpyCloud watchlist data with severity >= ', string(parameters('spycloudSeverityThreshold')), '. Triggers MDE device isolation and Entra ID password reset playbooks via automation rule.')]
properties.query:
[concat('SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= ', string(parameters('spycloudSeverityThreshold')), '
| where isnotempty(infected_machine_id)
| summarize
    ExposureCount = count(),
    Emails = make_set(email, 10),
    TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10),
    MaxSeverity = max(severity),
    LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated)
  by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, source_id
| extend
    HostCustomEntity = user_hostname,
    AccountCustomEntity = tostring(Emails[0])')]

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Data Ingestion Health Alert
properties.description: Fires when no SpyCloud data has been received for more than 2 hours, indicating a potential connector failure or API key issue.

allOf.query: union isfuzzy=true (datatable(LastRecord:datetime)[]) , (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | summarize LastRecord = max(TimeGenerated) | where LastRecord < ago(3h) or isnull(LastRecord))

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Infostealer Credential Exposure Detected
properties.description: Detects new infostealer-sourced credential exposures (severity >= 20) from SpyCloud. These indicate a device was actively infected with malware that stole credentials, cookies, and session tokens. Severity 25 includes stolen application data enabling MFA bypass.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(infected_machine_id)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), Emails = make_set(email, 10), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10), MaxSeverity = max(severity) by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, source_id
| extend HostCustomEntity = user_hostname, AccountCustomEntity = tostring(Emails[0])

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Plaintext Password Exposure (Critical Risk)
properties.description: Fires when a SpyCloud exposure record contains a cleartext plaintext password. These credentials can be used immediately by attackers without any cracking. Requires urgent password reset.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(password_plaintext)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10), LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated) by email, email_domain
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — SSN / Financial / Health Data Exposure (Compliance Alert)
properties.description: Detects exposure records containing Social Security numbers, bank account numbers, tax IDs, or health insurance IDs. May trigger breach notification obligations under HIPAA, PCI DSS, GDPR, or state privacy laws.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(social_security_number) or isnotempty(ssn_last_four) or isnotempty(bank_number) or isnotempty(taxid) or isnotempty(health_insurance_id)
| summarize PIITypes = make_set(case(isnotempty(social_security_number), 'SSN-Full', isnotempty(ssn_last_four), 'SSN-Last4', isnotempty(bank_number), 'BankAccount', isnotempty(taxid), 'TaxID', isnotempty(health_insurance_id), 'HealthInsurance', 'Other'), 10), ExposureCount = count() by email, full_name
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Stolen Session Cookies / Tokens (MFA Bypass Risk)
properties.description: Detects severity 25 exposures which include stolen browser cookies, session tokens, and autofill data. Attackers can replay these tokens to bypass MFA entirely. Requires immediate session revocation for all affected users.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity == 25
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 15), Devices = make_set(infected_machine_id, 5) by email, user_hostname
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, HostCustomEntity = user_hostname

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Device Re-Infection Detected
properties.description: Detects when the same infected_machine_id appears in new SpyCloud exposure records after a previous remediation was logged in the MDE table. Indicates the device was re-compromised after initial remediation, suggesting incomplete cleanup or persistent malware.
properties.query:
let remediated_devices = Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL | where IsolationStatus == true | distinct InfectedMachineId;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where infected_machine_id in (remediated_devices)
| summarize NewExposures = count(), LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated) by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, email
| extend HostCustomEntity = user_hostname, AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — User Credentials Exposed Across Multiple Domains
properties.description: Detects when a single user has credentials stolen for 5 or more different target domains, indicating widespread credential reuse or a heavily infected device. High risk of account takeover across multiple services.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| summarize TargetDomainCount = dcount(target_domain), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 20), MaxSeverity = max(severity), HasPlaintext = countif(isnotempty(password_plaintext)) by email
| where TargetDomainCount >= 5
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Infection from Unusual Geographic Location
properties.description: Detects infostealer infections from country codes that are uncommon in your environment (fewer than 3 previous infections from that country). May indicate targeted attacks, compromised travel devices, or unauthorized access from unexpected locations.
properties.query:
let country_baseline = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | summarize DeviceCount = dcount(infected_machine_id) by country_code | where DeviceCount >= 3 | distinct country_code;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(country_code)
| where country_code !in (country_baseline)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), Emails = make_set(email, 10) by country_code, user_hostname, infected_machine_id
| extend HostCustomEntity = user_hostname

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Credential Observed in Multiple Breach Sources
properties.description: Detects credentials with a sighting count of 3 or more, meaning the same email/password combination has been observed across multiple independent breach sources. This dramatically increases the probability of active credential stuffing attacks using this pair.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where sighting >= 3
| summarize MaxSighting = max(sighting), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10), PasswordTypes = make_set(password_type, 5), LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated) by email
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — High-Severity Exposure Without Automated Remediation
properties.description: Detects severity 20+ infostealer exposures that have been in the watchlist for over 2 hours but have NO corresponding entry in the Conditional Access remediation logs. This means the automated password reset playbook did not trigger, leaving the compromised credential active.
properties.query:
let remediated_users = SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL | distinct UserEmail;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where TimeGenerated < ago(2h)
| where email !in (remediated_users)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), MaxSeverity = max(severity), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10) by email, infected_machine_id
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Antivirus Present But Failed to Prevent Infostealer
properties.description: Detects infostealer infections on devices that had antivirus software installed, indicating the AV product failed to prevent the malware. Useful for evaluating endpoint protection effectiveness and identifying products that need updated signatures or configuration changes.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(av_softwares)
| mv-expand av_softwares
| summarize InfectedDevices = dcount(infected_machine_id), AffectedUsers = dcount(email) by tostring(av_softwares)
| where InfectedDevices >= 2

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — New Malware Family Appeared in Breach Catalog
properties.description: Fires when a new breach catalog entry appears with a status of 'pending' (newly ingested). This means SpyCloud has identified a new breach or malware source that may contain your organization's data. Early awareness enables proactive response before full ingestion completes.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL
| where status == 'pending'
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| project TimeGenerated, source_id, breach_title, description, status

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Data Ingestion Health Alert
properties.description: Fires when no new SpyCloud watchlist data has been received for more than 3 hours, indicating a potential connector failure, API key expiration, or rate limiting issue. Check SentinelHealth table and the SpyCloud connector page for errors.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| summarize LastRecord = max(TimeGenerated)
| where LastRecord < ago(3h) or isnull(LastRecord)
| extend AlertMessage = strcat('Last SpyCloud data received: ', tostring(LastRecord))

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Okta — Compromised Credential Used in Okta Sign-In
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud exposed email addresses with Okta sign-in events in Sentinel. Fires when a user with known compromised credentials (from SpyCloud) successfully authenticates through Okta, indicating the stolen credentials may be actively exploited. Requires Okta logs ingested via the Okta SSO connector in Sentinel.
properties.query:
let compromised_users = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | distinct email;
Okta_CL
| where eventType_s == 'user.session.start' and outcome_result_s == 'SUCCESS'
| where actor_alternateId_s in (compromised_users)
| extend AccountCustomEntity = actor_alternateId_s, IPCustomEntity = client_ipAddress_s

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Duo — Compromised Credential in Duo Authentication
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud exposed emails with Cisco Duo authentication logs. Fires when a user with known compromised credentials authenticates through Duo. Requires Duo Admin API logs ingested into Sentinel.
properties.query:
let compromised_users = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | distinct email;
Duo_CL
| where result_s == 'SUCCESS'
| where email_s in (compromised_users)
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email_s, IPCustomEntity = access_device_ip_s

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Ping Identity — Compromised Credential in PingOne Auth
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud exposed emails with Ping Identity/PingOne authentication logs. Requires PingOne logs ingested into Sentinel via syslog or API connector.
properties.query:
let compromised_users = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | distinct email;
PingFederate_CL
| where status_s == 'success'
| where subject_s in (compromised_users)
| extend AccountCustomEntity = subject_s, IPCustomEntity = ip_s

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Entra ID — Compromised Credential Sign-In Detected
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud exposed emails with Entra ID (Azure AD) sign-in logs. Fires when a user with known compromised credentials from SpyCloud successfully signs in, especially from unusual locations or with anomalous patterns. Requires Entra ID sign-in logs enabled (enableEntraSignInLogs=true).
properties.query:
let compromised_users = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | distinct email;
SigninLogs
| where ResultType == 0
| where UserPrincipalName in (compromised_users)
| summarize SignInCount = count(), Locations = make_set(Location, 5), IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5) by UserPrincipalName, AppDisplayName
| extend AccountCustomEntity = UserPrincipalName

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Compromised Credential with Recent Entra Sign-In
properties.description: Detects when a user whose credentials were exposed by SpyCloud has successfully signed into Entra ID within the last 24 hours. This indicates the compromised credential may still be active and the account is at immediate risk of takeover.
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | distinct email;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where ResultType == 0
| where UserPrincipalName in (exposed)
| summarize SignInCount = count(), IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5), Apps = make_set(AppDisplayName, 5) by UserPrincipalName
| extend AccountCustomEntity = UserPrincipalName

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — New Exposures with Breach Source Context
properties.description: Enriches new SpyCloud watchlist records with breach catalog details (breach title, description) by joining on source_id. Fires when new high-severity exposures arrive from known breach sources, providing SOC analysts with the full context of where the data came from.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where severity >= 20
| join kind=leftouter (SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL | project source_id, breach_title, description, status) on source_id
| where isnotempty(breach_title)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), Emails = make_set(email, 10) by source_id, breach_title, status
| extend AccountCustomEntity = tostring(Emails[0])

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Executive / VIP Account Exposure
properties.description: Detects exposures for email addresses that match a predefined list of executive or VIP accounts. Customize the 'vip_domains' list in the KQL to match your executive team's email patterns. These exposures require immediate C-suite notification and priority remediation.
properties.query:
let vip_patterns = dynamic(['ceo@','cfo@','cto@','ciso@','coo@','president@','vp-','director-']);
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 5
| where email has_any (vip_patterns) or email_username in ('admin','administrator','root','sysadmin')
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), MaxSeverity = max(severity), Sources = make_set(source_id, 5) by email, full_name
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Same Password Reused Across Multiple Target Domains
properties.description: Detects when the same password hash appears in exposures targeting 3 or more different domains for the same user. This indicates dangerous credential reuse — if one account is compromised, all accounts with the same password are at risk.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(password) or isnotempty(password_plaintext)
| extend pw_hash = hash_sha256(coalesce(password_plaintext, password))
| summarize TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 20), DomainCount = dcount(target_domain) by email, pw_hash
| where DomainCount >= 3
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Exposure Older Than 7 Days Without Remediation
properties.description: Detects high-severity (20+) exposures that have been in the watchlist for more than 7 days but have no corresponding entry in either the MDE or CA remediation logs. This is a compliance and SLA alert — these exposures should have been remediated within your organization's defined response window.
properties.query:
let mde_remediated = Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL | distinct InfectedMachineId;
let ca_remediated = SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL | distinct UserEmail;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where TimeGenerated < ago(7d)
| where infected_machine_id !in (mde_remediated)
| where email !in (ca_remediated)
| summarize DaysExposed = datetime_diff('day', now(), min(TimeGenerated)), ExposureCount = count() by email, infected_machine_id
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.scriptContent:
#!/bin/bash
# SpyCloud Sentinel — Post-Deployment (all steps non-fatal)
# If the managed identity lacks permissions, steps will skip gracefully.
# Run verify-deployment.sh from Cloud Shell to check + fix anything missed.

echo "SpyCloud Sentinel — Post-Deployment"

echo "[1/7] Waiting 90s for resource provisioning..."
sleep 90

echo "[2/7] Resolving DCE..."
DCE_URI=$(az monitor data-collection endpoint show --name "$DCE_NAME" -g "$RG" --query "logsIngestion.endpoint" -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
for i in 1 2 3 4; do
  [ -n "$DCE_URI" ] && break
  sleep 20
  DCE_URI=$(az monitor data-collection endpoint show --name "$DCE_NAME" -g "$RG" --query "logsIngestion.endpoint" -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
done
echo "  DCE=${DCE_URI:-NOT_RESOLVED}"

echo "[3/7] Resolving DCR..."
DCR_ID=""
DCR_RID=""
for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  DCR_ID=$(az monitor data-collection rule show --name "$DCR_NAME" -g "$RG" --query "immutableId" -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
  DCR_RID=$(az monitor data-collection rule show --name "$DCR_NAME" -g "$RG" --query "id" -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
  [ -n "$DCR_ID" ] && break
  sleep 20
done
echo "  DCR=${DCR_ID:-NOT_RESOLVED}"

echo "[4/7] Assigning Monitoring Metrics Publisher..."
if [ -n "$DCR_RID" ]; then
  for PB in $MDE_PB $CA_PB $CRED_PB $BL_PB; do
    PID=$(az logic workflow show --name "$PB" -g "$RG" --query "identity.principalId" -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
    if [ -n "$PID" ]; then
      az role assignment create --assignee-object-id "$PID" --assignee-principal-type ServicePrincipal         --role "3913510d-42f4-4e42-8a64-420c390055eb" --scope "$DCR_RID" 2>/dev/null         && echo "  $PB ✓" || echo "  $PB: exists or no permission"
    fi
  done
fi

echo "[5/7] MDE API permissions..."
MDE_SP=$(az ad sp show --id "fc780465-2017-40d4-a0c5-307022471b92" --query "id" -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
if [ -n "$MDE_SP" ]; then
  for PB in "$MDE_PB" "$BL_PB"; do
    PID=$(az logic workflow show --name "$PB" -g "$RG" --query "identity.principalId" -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
    [ -z "$PID" ] && continue
    for PERM in "Machine.Isolate" "Machine.ReadWrite.All"; do
      RID=$(az ad sp show --id "fc780465-2017-40d4-a0c5-307022471b92" --query "appRoles[?value=='$PERM'].id | [0]" -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
      [ -n "$RID" ] && az rest --method POST         --uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/$MDE_SP/appRoleAssignedTo"         --body "{\"principalId\":\"$PID\",\"resourceId\":\"$MDE_SP\",\"appRoleId\":\"$RID\"}"         2>/dev/null && echo "  $PERM → $PB ✓" || echo "  $PERM: exists or no permission"
    done
  done
fi

echo "[6/7] Graph API permissions..."
GRAPH_SP=$(az ad sp show --id "00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000" --query "id" -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
if [ -n "$GRAPH_SP" ]; then
  for PB in "$CA_PB" "$CRED_PB"; do
    PID=$(az logic workflow show --name "$PB" -g "$RG" --query "identity.principalId" -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
    [ -z "$PID" ] && continue
    for PERM in "User.ReadWrite.All" "Directory.ReadWrite.All"; do
      RID=$(az ad sp show --id "00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000" --query "appRoles[?value=='$PERM'].id | [0]" -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
      [ -n "$RID" ] && az rest --method POST         --uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/$GRAPH_SP/appRoleAssignedTo"         --body "{\"principalId\":\"$PID\",\"resourceId\":\"$GRAPH_SP\",\"appRoleId\":\"$RID\"}"         2>/dev/null && echo "  $PERM → $PB ✓" || echo "  $PERM: exists or no permission"
    done
  done
fi

echo "[7/7] Automation rule..."
MDE_LA=$(az logic workflow show --name "$MDE_PB" -g "$RG" --query id -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
WS_ID=$(az monitor log-analytics workspace show --workspace-name "$WS" -g "$RG" --query id -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
TID=$(az account show --query tenantId -o tsv 2>/dev/null) || true
if [ -n "$MDE_LA" ] && [ -n "$WS_ID" ]; then
  az rest --method PUT     --uri "https://management.azure.com${WS_ID}/providers/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/automationRules/spycloud-auto-rule?api-version=2023-02-01-preview"     --body "{\"properties\":{\"displayName\":\"SpyCloud Auto Remediation\",\"order\":1,\"triggeringLogic\":{\"isEnabled\":true,\"triggersOn\":\"Incidents\",\"triggersWhen\":\"Created\",\"conditions\":[]},\"actions\":[{\"order\":1,\"actionType\":\"RunPlaybook\",\"actionConfiguration\":{\"logicAppResourceId\":\"$MDE_LA\",\"tenantId\":\"$TID\"}}]}}"     2>/dev/null && echo "  Automation rule ✓" || echo "  Automation rule: needs manual setup"
fi

echo ""
echo "DONE. DCE=$DCE_URI DCR=$DCR_ID"
echo "Verify: curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL/main/scripts/verify-deployment.sh | bash -s -- -g $RG -w $WS"

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × DNS — Infected User Resolving Malicious Domains
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × DNS — Infected User Resolving Malicious Domains
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20
| distinct email, target_domain;
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where Name has_any (exposed | project target_domain)
| join kind=inner exposed on $left.Name == $right.target_domain
| project TimeGenerated, ClientIP, Name, email, target_domain, QueryType

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Network — Infected IP in Device Network Events
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × Network — Infected IP in Device Network Events
properties.query:
let ips = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20
| mv-expand ip = ip_addresses
| extend src_ip = tostring(ip)
| where isnotempty(src_ip)
| distinct src_ip, email, infected_machine_id;
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where RemoteIP in~ (ips | project src_ip)
| join kind=inner ips on $left.RemoteIP == $right.src_ip
| project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, email, infected_machine_id

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × MDE Devices — Infected Machine in Defender Inventory
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × MDE Devices — Infected Machine in Defender Inventory
properties.query:
let devices = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20 and isnotempty(user_hostname)
| distinct user_hostname, email, severity, infected_machine_id;
DeviceInfo
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| join kind=inner devices on $left.DeviceName == $right.user_hostname
| project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, DeviceId, OSPlatform, email, severity, infected_machine_id

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × MDE Alerts — Exposure Correlated with Defender Alert
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × MDE Alerts — Exposure Correlated with Defender Alert
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 5
| distinct email;
AlertInfo
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2d)
| join kind=inner AlertEvidence on AlertId
| where EntityType == 'User'
| extend upn = tolower(strcat(AccountName, '@', AccountDomain))
| where upn in~ (exposed)
| project TimeGenerated, AlertId, Title, Severity, upn, Category

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Cloud Apps — Compromised User Accessing SaaS
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × Cloud Apps — Compromised User Accessing SaaS
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 5
| distinct email;
CloudAppEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2d)
| where AccountId in~ (exposed)
| where ActionType in ('FileDownloaded','FileUploaded','FileShared','MailSend')
| summarize Actions=count(), Apps=dcount(Application) by AccountId, Application, ActionType

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Entra Risk — Exposed User Flagged Risky
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × Entra Risk — Exposed User Flagged Risky
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 5
| distinct email;
AADUserRiskEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2d)
| where UserPrincipalName in~ (exposed)
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, RiskLevel, RiskState, RiskDetail

value.step1_mde_permissions: REQUIRED (if MDE playbook enabled): Assign Machine.Isolate + Machine.ReadWrite.All to the MDE Logic App managed identity via PowerShell
value.step2_graph_permissions: REQUIRED (if CA playbook enabled): Assign User.ReadWrite.All + Directory.ReadWrite.All to the CA Logic App managed identity via PowerShell
value.step3_sentinel_automation: REQUIRED (if automation rule enabled): Assign 'Microsoft Sentinel Automation Contributor' to the Sentinel service principal
value.step4_monitoring_publisher: REQUIRED (if playbooks enabled): Assign 'Monitoring Metrics Publisher' on the DCR to both Logic App identities
value.step5_key_vault_access: OPTIONAL: If using Key Vault with Logic Apps, assign Key Vault Secrets User role to Logic App identities
value.step6_defender_xdr: OPTIONAL: Enable Microsoft Defender XDR connector via Sentinel → Content Hub
value.step7_copilot_plugin: OPTIONAL: Upload SpyCloud_Copilot_Plugin_Ultimate.yaml to Security Copilot → Sources → Custom
value.step8_copilot_agent: OPTIONAL: Upload SpyCloud_Agent_Manifest.yaml via Security Copilot → Build → Upload YAML manifest
value.step0_dcr_dce: REQUIRED (if playbooks enabled): Get the DCE Logs Ingestion URI and DCR Immutable ID. Go to Azure Portal → Monitor → Data Collection Endpoints → copy Logs Ingestion URI. Then Monitor → Data Collection Rules → JSON View → copy immutableId. Update the Logic App parameters with these values, or redeploy the template with them filled in.

value.note: Run scripts/post-deploy.sh to auto-resolve DCE URI and DCR Immutable ID
ALL rules are deployed DISABLED. Review each rule's KQL logic, then enable individually in Sentinel → Analytics.
value.idpNote: Identity Provider correlation rules (Okta, Duo, Ping, Entra) require those IdP logs to be ingested into Sentinel first via their respective Content Hub connectors.

value.okta: To ingest Okta logs: Sentinel → Content Hub → search 'Okta' → Install → Configure with Okta API token. Table: Okta_CL
value.duo: To ingest Duo logs: Sentinel → Content Hub → search 'Cisco Duo' → Install → Configure with Duo Admin API credentials. Table: Duo_CL
value.ping: To ingest Ping Identity logs: Configure syslog forwarding from PingFederate to Sentinel via AMA agent, or use the PingOne API connector. Table: PingFederate_CL
value.cyberark: To ingest CyberArk logs: Sentinel → Content Hub → search 'CyberArk' → Install → Configure CEF/syslog forwarding. Table: CyberArk_CL
value.entraId: Entra ID sign-in/audit logs: Enable via this template's enableEntraSignInLogs and enableEntraAuditLogs parameters, or manually via Entra ID → Diagnostic Settings.
value.defenderXdr: Defender XDR: Enable via Sentinel → Content Hub → Microsoft Defender XDR → Install & Connect. Provides AlertInfo, DeviceEvents, IdentityLogonEvents tables.
value.exchangeOnline: Exchange/O365: Enable via Sentinel → Content Hub → Microsoft 365 → Install. Provides OfficeActivity, EmailEvents tables for phishing correlation.

value.note: Entra ID diagnostic settings CANNOT be deployed via ARM templates. Configure manually:
value.signInLogs: Entra ID → Monitoring → Diagnostic settings → Add diagnostic setting → check SignInLogs, NonInteractiveUserSignInLogs → Send to Log Analytics workspace → select your workspace
value.auditLogs: Entra ID → Monitoring → Diagnostic settings → Add diagnostic setting → check AuditLogs → Send to Log Analytics workspace → select your workspace
value.riskySignIns: Entra ID → Monitoring → Diagnostic settings → Add diagnostic setting → check RiskyUsers, UserRiskEvents → Send to Log Analytics workspace

value.note: Run scripts/post-deploy.sh after deployment to resolve DCE/DCR and assign RBAC + API permissions.

value.name: [variables('credResponsePlaybookName')]
value.permissions: Security Administrator, Graph: IdentityRisk.ReadWrite.All, User.ReadWrite.All, AuditLog.Read.All

value.name: SpyCloud Threat Intelligence Dashboard
value.location: Sentinel → Workbooks → My workbooks → SpyCloud Threat Intelligence Dashboard

value.note: Log Analytics workspace and Sentinel workspace are the SAME resource. Sentinel is a solution installed on top of the Log Analytics workspace.
